TimeEntry.Title: BW-ACCT02,HQ-OPSPRB-01,BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-LT-01 : Seen critical error in OP manager
TimeEntry.Notes:
BW-ACCT02:
Disk utilization was above 75%. The C: drive was cleaned, and disk usage is now within acceptable limits.

BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01:
An incorrect network interface was selected. The wrong interface was removed and the correct Ethernet interface corresponding to the IP address was added. Memory utilization is now optimized.

BWH-HQ-APP-01:
An incorrect network interface was selected for communication. The wrong interface was removed and the correct Ethernet interface with the appropriate IP address was added. The C: drive was also cleaned, reducing disk usage to below the threshold (60%).

BWH-HQ-LT-01:
An incorrect interface was selected. The machine was removed and re-added, ensuring only the correct Ethernet IP address with SNMP was selected.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-Vlan Network Segmentation Plan
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: BWH Network Plan Update

Ticket Title: Update Network Plan with Cloudbrink and Access Points in Desktop VLAN

Summary:

The network plan for BWH was updated to incorporate Cloudbrink for secure remote access and Access Points in VLAN 2 (Desktop & Server VLAN). The changes include:

  1. VLAN Configuration:

    • VLAN 1 (Management) with IP range 10.1.1.0/24

    • VLAN 7 (Backup) with IP range 10.1.7.0/24

    • VLAN 2 (Desktop & Server) with IP range 10.1.2.0/24 and DHCP for desktops and servers.

  2. Cloudbrink Setup:

    • Cloudbrink was installed for secure remote access, allowing users to log in remotely and access resources from any VLAN (Management, Backup, Desktop).

    • User access control and permissions have been defined in Cloudbrink.

  3. Access Points Configuration:

    • Access Points are now connected to VLAN 2 (Desktop & Server VLAN), allowing wireless devices to receive IPs from the same DHCP range as desktops and servers.

    • Static IPs have been assigned to each access point:

      • BWH-HQ-AP-01: 10.1.2.81

      • BWH-HQ-AP-02: 10.1.2.82

      • BWH-HQ-AP-03: 10.1.2.83

      • BWH-HQ-AP-04: 10.1.2.84

      • BWH-HQ-AP-05: 10.1.2.85

  4. Device Review:

    • Printers and other physical devices connected to VLAN 2 were reviewed and verified to be within the DHCP range 10.1.2.90 - 10.1.2.225.

  5. Backup Plan:

    • Device configurations for Cisco Switch and Sophos Firewall have been backed up to restore settings if needed.

    • In case of network failure, Cloudbrink ensures that remote access remains functional.

Action Taken:

  • Configuration of VLANs on Sophos Firewall and Cisco Switch.

  • Setup of Cloudbrink for remote user access.

  • Deployment of Access Points in VLAN 2 and ensuring wireless clients are assigned IPs within the Desktop VLAN range.

  • Reviewed and tested physical devices connected to VLAN 2.

TimeEntry.Title: Worked with Rishad as he is onsite to change the UPS and Server Battery
TimeEntry.Notes:
As Rishad was on-site at BWH to replace the old UPS with a new one and to change the HP Smart Battery in the server, I logged into the ESXi host and powered off all the VMs one by one. Once all VMs were safely shut down, I powered off the ESXi server. After confirming the server was offline, I requested Rishad to unplug the power cables and proceed with the battery and UPS replacement.
Once the battery replacement was completed, he powered the server back on. I verified the server’s status through iLO and then logged into the ESXi host to ensure that all VMs were powered on. I accessed each VM individually to confirm that all services were running properly.
The same process was followed for the QNAP device. I powered it off before the UPS replacement, and after the new UPS was installed, we verified that the QNAP came back online and was accessible without any issues.
Next, I accessed the network switch through PuTTY and took a backup of its configuration. After the backup was completed, the switch was powered off and moved to the new UPS. It came back online after a few minutes. Some backup jobs initially failed, but after troubleshooting, everything is now functioning normally.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: this file name
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received an email from Sandy regarding the file name length issue where she was unable to rename certain files from her OneDrive desktop.

Connected to her computer remotely to troubleshoot the issue. Attempted to rename the files directly from File Explorer; however, due to the long file path limitation (OneDrive sync + deep folder structure), the files could not be renamed from the desktop application.

Assisted Sandy in accessing OneDrive via the web interface as a workaround. Provided the following steps:

  1. Open a web browser (Chrome/Edge).

  2. Go to https://www.office.com.

  3. Sign in with her work email account.

  4. Click on OneDrive from the app launcher.

  5. Navigate to the affected folder path.

  6. Right-click the file and select Rename.

From the OneDrive web portal, she was successfully able to edit and rename the files. Assisted her in renaming the files one by one to shorten the names and ensure they comply with path length limitations.

Confirmed that the files were updated successfully and syncing properly after the changes.

Sent a follow-up email to Sandy confirming that the issue has been resolved and advised her to reach out if any further assistance is needed.

Issue resolved at this time.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-06. Disk Utilization is 99%, threshold value for this monitor is 90%
TimeEntry.Notes: We received an alert on My OPS regarding disk utilization for BWH-HQ-LT-06 under the username Rwood, which was at 99% capacity, well above the threshold. Upon logging into the system, I found the disk utilization to be at 99%. I started by performing a Disk Cleanup, clearing Windows log files, temporary files, and templates. I also deleted temp files from the Temp% and Prefetch folders. After this, 20 GB was cleared. Additionally, I removed older Bluebeam setups from 2022 and 2024, which accounted for another significant chunk of wasted space (3-4 files of 2 GB each). These actions freed up space and improved the system's performance, ensuring smoother operation. Ticket closed after successful cleanup.

TimeEntry.Title: Dynamic Group Creation & Huntress Learner Sync Configuration for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: Configured a new Microsoft Entra ID Dynamic User Group specifically designed to include only licensed mailbox-enabled users. Created the group using a validated dynamic membership rule that filters for active, member-type accounts with an enabled Exchange Online service plan, ensuring only real, licensed users are included. After successfully creating and verifying the dynamic group membership logic, proceeded to integrate Huntress SAT with Microsoft Graph. In the Huntress portal, navigated to Integrations → Providers, selected the BWH tenant, and chose the newly created dynamic user group (BWH_Users) as the group to sync learners from. Enabled the option to exclude unlicensed and non-human learner identities, ensuring only properly licensed human users can be provisioned as Huntress learners. Confirmed that Use UPN as unique email address was selected to maintain identity accuracy. Activated Automatic Daily Sync so learner accounts are continuously updated based on group membership changes. Also enabled attribute sharing options (Status, First Name, Last Name, Employee ID, Department, Manager) and verified advanced settings related to learner activation/inactivation and department creation. Completed validation of the sync configuration to ensure that only authorized, licensed users will populate within Huntress SAT moving forward.

TimeEntry.Title: Trash bin
TimeEntry.Notes:
Jody encountered two issues: one with the Recycle Bin and another with email delivery delays.

  1. Recycle Bin Issue: Jody was unable to restore or delete items from the Recycle Bin. To resolve this, I logged into his computer, opened Command Prompt as Administrator, and used the command to clear the Recycle Bin.

  2. Email Delivery Delay: Jody was experiencing a delay in email delivery. Emails were taking more than 10 seconds to be sent. I logged into his Outlook, identified the issue, ended the Outlook task, and proceeded to repair Outlook. After the repair, I tested the email by sending a test message to our support , which went through successfully.

  3. I then asked Jody to send a test email from his side for confirmation, and he confirmed that the email was delivered promptly.

With Jody's confirmation that the issues were resolved, I am closing this ticket.
